description_long: This sculpture features an abstract, organic form with a central circular ring structure, from which several branch-like extensions emerge. Three vertical, elongated elements resembling stylized animal heads or totems are positioned within the lower half of the ring, facing outward. Two prominent, curved arms extend horizontally from the base, each ending in a rounded knob, giving the piece a sense of symmetry and balance. The surface appears rough and irregular, with a metallic, weathered patina that suggests age and exposure to the elements. The sculpture is mounted on a flat, rectangular black plinth, providing stability and contrast to the intricate upper structure. The overall impression is one of dynamic movement and natural inspiration, evoking antlers, branches, or ancient ritual objects.
description: An abstract metallic sculpture with a circular ring, branch-like extensions, and three vertical totemic forms, mounted on a black plinth. The piece has a rough, weathered surface and symmetrical, curved arms.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular ring, three vertical totemic forms, and symmetrical curved arms extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth of the sculpture, showing the thickness of the ring and the staggered arrangement of the vertical forms.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the ring and three vertical forms visible, but the details of the faces are less pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the curvature of the arms and the layered structure of the vertical elements.
attribute_subject_matter: abstract organic forms with totemic elements
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a black plinth, suggesting an outdoor urban or park setting, but no specific environmental context is visible.
